mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses below 9.0%, it signals a labor market inflection point that boosts consumer confidence and aggregate demand, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 50% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 9.0%, or the signal's predictive power decays to zero within 6 months of the crossing.

mechanism_narrative: The joint acceleration of housing starts and revolving consumer credit indicates a synchronized recovery in durable goods spending and household balance sheet repair, which positively drives equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term (HOUST momentum × RSAFS momentum) shows a negative or statistically insignificant correlation with 60-day forward SPX returns across three consecutive economic cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth exceeding its 12-month moving average signals robust corporate capacity utilization and pricing power, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next week as earnings revisions turn upward.
failure_condition: Rank IC of INDPRO momentum relative to its 12-month MA drops below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns are flat when INDPRO outpaces its trend.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth consistently above 2.5% annualized reflects underlying economic momentum that supports corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon regardless of valuation levels.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns show no statistical difference between periods of GDPC1 growth above 2.5% and periods below 2.5% over a 10-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the CPI exceeding 0.5% triggers inflation hedging flows and anticipates tighter monetary policy, which compresses equity multiples and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of instances following a CPI MoM print above 0.5%, or the signal's directional accuracy falls below 52% over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a self-reinforcing labor market recovery, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon as risk premiums compress.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark over any 3-year rolling window, or the interaction coefficient becomes statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts accelerating above 500 thousand units signals a bottom in the residential investment cycle, which historically precedes broad equity market rallies over the next month as construction supply chains reactivate.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are negative or flat in > 55% of months where HOUST exceeds 500k units, or the signal's information coefficient decays to zero after the 2008 financial crisis.
